ogTitle: Cambridge Gulf vs Cancanning: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cambridge Gulf and Cancanning.
heading: Cambridge Gulf vs Cancanning — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cambridge Gulf, Western Australia and Cancanning, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cambridge Gulf has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Cancanning (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cambridge Gulf or Cancanning safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cambridge Gulf has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Cancanning scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cambridge Gulf scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Cancanning scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cambridge Gulf or Cancanning?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cambridge Gulf has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
